title: Sales Enablement Specialist I, Amazon Global Logistics
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


Join Amazon Global Logistics as we revolutionize international transportation for millions of sellers worldwide. As a Sales Enablement Specialist, you'll deliver training programs that support sales team performance across the US and EMEA, helping break down barriers between buyers and sellers globally.  

  

Amazon Global Logistics (AGL) provides international transportation services for FBA Sellers, offering complete supply chain solutions. You will support training programs for our sales teams, contribute to new product launch enablement, and collaborate with cross\-functional partners to remove sales obstacles. Your primary focus will be supporting sales team productivity through effective training delivery, streamlined processes, and tool adoption.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Deliver training sessions for Amazon Global Logistics teams, covering sales strategies, customer engagement, and internal tools for both new and experienced employees
* Develop engaging training materials using various methods including live sessions and eLearning resources to accommodate different learning styles
* Identify obstacles that prevent sales teams from achieving their goals and support initiatives to address these challenges through targeted training programs
* Collaborate with AGL team members to support initiatives, tools, and materials including online guides, reference documents, and e\-learning content
* Partner with diverse stakeholders including Sales Leadership, Sellers, and Sales Operations to gather insights and support training effectiveness
* Support the establishment of measurable targets for training and onboarding that align with sales objectives, monitor program effectiveness through data analysis, and recommend adjustments based on performance metrics

  

A day in the life  

You'll start your day reviewing training effectiveness metrics and sales team feedback. You might spend your morning facilitating a live training session for new AGL team members on customer engagement strategies, then collaborate with Sales Leadership in the afternoon to identify skill gaps and support targeted interventions. You'll work closely with Sales Operations, Product teams, and regional sales managers to ensure training content stays current with product launches and market changes. Your work directly supports how effectively our sales teams serve Amazon's global selling partners.  

  

About the team  

Global Logistics is responsible for providing FBA Sellers a one stop shop for international transportation service, providing end to end supply chain solutions for our Customers. Eliminating borders for buyers and sellers, Global Logistics facilitates Amazon's expansion into a rapidly growing list of countries, creating new logistical benchmarks and "firsts" through disruptive innovation. Global Logistics is a multicultural, multilingual team that works with merchants, sellers, providers, governments and trade officials from the busiest to the most remote locations around the world, applying a global perspective with a local approach. Our mission is "Every supplier. Every location. Every customer on Earth".**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**

* 1\+ years of using a CRM (Customer Relationship Management) tool experience

* Experience defining, refining and implementing sales processes, procedures and policies or equivalent

* Experience in program or project management
* PMP certificate

title: Sr Program Manager, Powered Industrial Truck (PIT) Training , Safety Learning and Development
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


Amazon is seeking an experienced and enthusiastic Senior Training Manager to lead global Powered Industrial Truck (PIT) Training within the Workplace Health \& Safety (WHS) organization.
  

This position offers the chance to modernize and scale PIT training worldwide, building mechanisms that strengthen safety outcomes and support compliance across Operations. The Sr. Mgr will own the launch of new PIT training content, oversee continuous improvement, and guide change management efforts. A key part of the role is ensuring all training programs, materials, and procedures meet global regulatory requirements and internal compliance standards, and that updates are implemented consistently across regions.  

You will work cross\-functionally with WHS, Operations, Learning, Instructional Designers, and global business partners to design, deploy, audit, and refine PIT training. This includes monitoring regulatory changes, validating alignment with compliance expectations, and partnering with legal and policy teams as needed.  

Success in this role requires strong analytical acumen, clear communication, and the ability to influence leaders at every level. You will build strategy, drive execution, develop KPIs, and continually identify opportunities to improve adoption, effectiveness, compliance, and overall operational excellence.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Lead the global strategy, development, launch, and maintenance of PIT training programs, maintaining alignment with regulatory requirements and internal compliance standards.
* Serve as the global subject\-matter expert (SME) for PIT training, acting as the primary point of contact for compliance expectations across regions and business units.
* Design and execute global training roadmaps, including gap analyses, standard setting, and long\-term regulatory readiness planning.
* Lead change management efforts for new and updated PIT training, driving consistent implementation, adoption, and regulatory alignment worldwide.
* Establish scalable communication mechanisms to support training launches, program updates, and compliance or regulatory changes.
* Audit PIT training procedures, documentation, and materials, partnering with national and global teams to achieve accuracy, consistency, and regulatory adherence.
* Build and maintain strong cross\-functional partnerships with WHS, Operations, Learning, Legal, and global stakeholders to embed training into operational rhythms and strengthen safety culture.
* Define and manage success metrics and KPIs, using data to measure program effectiveness, learner performance, compliance health, and business impact.
* Analyze trends and risks to identify compliance gaps and improvement opportunities, driving continuous improvement through stakeholder feedback and data insights.
* Provide clear leadership reporting through business reviews and status updates, highlighting progress, risks, compliance posture, and corrective actions.
**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* 5\+ years of program or project management experience
* Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
* Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
* 5\+ years of cross functional project delivery experience
* Experience working cross functionally with tech and non\-tech teams
* Bachelor’s degree in one of the following fields or similar: Occupational Health \& Safety, Industrial Engineering, Education, Adult Learning or Instructional Design, Business Management

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**

* Experience with safety programs, PIT operations, training development, or regulatory compliance

title: Immigration Specialist , Immigration
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


At Amazon, we are relentlessly focused on delighting our customers and fostering a culture of innovation. Our Immigration team is at the forefront of this mission, ensuring a seamless and compliant immigration, relocation, and tax experience for our global workforce and stakeholders.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Risk Assessment. The role is responsible for documenting risk associated with U.S. immigration. This includes:


o Engage with internal Legal and external counsel to review U.S. risks and update the risk framework.  

o Execute and assess control efficacy and continually feed results into risk framework.  

o Own and document U.S. compliance controls.
  

o Engage internal Legal and internal US subject matter experts to prioritize risks for control development.  

o Identify, develop, and continually improve key performance indicators that quantify adherence against immigration obligations.
  

o Run monthly cross\-functional meetings to inform leadership of changes in U.S. risks and drive to completion projects to reduce risk.
  

  

* Audit response:

o Maintain documents including corporate front desk protocols and PAF retrieval SOPs.
  

o Conduct and maintain training for corporate front desk staff nationally.
  

o Engage with four separate security organizations to implement and maintain response protocols.
  

o Lead responder to FDNS visits and Interested Party PAF requests.  

o Conduct audits to assess Public Access Files (PAF) and PERM Audit File completeness.
  

  

A day in the life  

The Immigration Specialist, U.S. Compliance is responsible for the identification and mitigation of regulatory risk within the U.S. immigration program and for ensuring the program’s risk framework is continually refreshed. The role will partner with internal Legal and external counsel to keep informed of new executive orders, proclamations, and regulatory changes as well as evolving adjudication standards to ensure programmatic and employee impact is properly quantified. The individual will also execute and assess efficacy of controls to identify areas for improvement and incorporate findings back into the risk framework.
  

  

The Immigration Specialist role is responsible for ensuring Amazon timely and successfully responds to Fraud Detection and National Security (FDNS) site visits as well as for audits and requests related to Department of Labor mandated Public Access Files (PAFs) or Labor Certification Audit Files. This will require the individual to engage with leadership of corporate office front desk staff, four separate security organizations, as well as a talent acquisition team to ensure continual response training and compliant document retention practices are followed. The Immigration Specialist will also own all documentation, protocols, and SOPs related to government visits and audits, including continuous improvement of these areas. This role will lead cross functional teams in conducing mock audits and quality assurance audits to ensure that all document retention obligations are met.
**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* Bachelor's degree
* Experience defining and executing program requirements and using data and metrics to determine improvements
* 3\+ years of experience in immigration, legal, compliance, or risk management roles

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Experience developing policies and supporting documentation

title: Employee Relations Manager, Ops HR-ER
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


The North American Employee Relations (ER) team supports Field Operations organizations across fulfillment and logistics business lanes. The central objective of our team is to ensure treatment of employees that is fair, respectful, and consistent with our framework of our core business values and objectives. To support that objective, this position’s responsibilities fall into proactive and reactive categories. We are seeking an ER Manager with a demonstrated ability to collaborate with and influence field operations managers, human resources, and contractors; to analyze multiple levels of employee and partner feedback to identify risks and gaps; to create, audit, and sustain positive workplace relations programs; and to design and conduct positive workplace relations training that drives best practices. The Manager also will work reactively on crisis\-oriented situations that typically can only be successfully addressed under severe time constraints. The individual will work reactively on employee relations issues that may arise at a single station, within a specific geographic region, or network\-wide. Balancing time between projects and crisis\-response is a critical component for success in the position.
  

  

The Employee Relations Manager will possess employee and labor relations expertise, excellent judgment, and highly\-developed analytical, interpersonal, and communication skills. The Manager will be comfortable both presenting to senior leaders and communicating with frontline employees. The successful Manager will be a strong project manager, capable of independently owning large\-scale, network\-wide projects. The Manager will quickly become an important “source of truth” regarding whether the organization is meeting its core values and principles.
  

  

The role will cover the Southern California region supporting the last mile of Amazon delivery services. This position will support sites across one or more operational regions so comfort with travel is key. The successful candidate must be capable of significant domestic travel, sometimes with short notice, up to 50% of the time. This position is also RTO required, but open to location throughout US.
  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Detect and Mitigate Risks: Apply employee and labor relations expertise to successfully identify trends, gaps, and challenges; execute solution\-focused strategies and programs to mitigate risks
* Crisis Management: Work reactively on crisis\-oriented partner issues under time limitations while exercising excellent judgment
* Project Management: Independently own large\-scale, network\-wide projects delivered on time that improve frontline partner experience
* Analyze and Interpret Data: Exercise strong analytical skills using data and anecdotes to develop persuasive narratives and recommendations; demonstrated ability to define and measure success of programs
* Communicate Effectively: Rapidly produce high\-quality written communications that concisely analyze problems and recommends solutions; design and deliver highly\-engaging training sessions
* Collaborate: Build constructive relationships with critical stakeholders to share information and influence change
* Problem Solve: Work independently with minimal supervision, in ambiguous situations, and persevere over internal and external barriers to drive resolutions
* Work at Scale: Launch and manage sustainable mechanisms for entire networks

  

**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* 7\+ years of employee relations, labor relations, or labor/employment law experience
* Knowledge of Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Word, SharePoint, and PowerPoint)
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent
* Experience supporting large hourly workforces
* Experience analyzing and using metrics to identify improvements in employee

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Advanced degree, JD, or other specialized training in labor/employee relations
* Experience building and launching programs that measurably impact positive engagement
* Works well in a fast\-paced environment, flexible, able to change direction quickly and manage projects through ambiguity and constant change
* Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail, able to juggle and prioritize multiple tasks simultaneously while delivering on commitments
* Excellent communication skills in written and verbal form; extensive public speaking, training, and/or leadership communication skills
* Solid sense of accountability, high\-quality judgment and sound decision\-making
* Ability to handle confidential information and escalate issues when appropriate
* Ability to quickly connect with people across a wide variety of cultural and socio\-economic backgrounds
* Fluency in multiple languages
* Previous experience managing operations

  

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.  

  

Los Angeles County applicants: Job duties for this position include: work safely and cooperatively with other employees, supervisors, and staff; adhere to standards of excellence despite stressful conditions; communicate effectively and respectfully with employees, supervisors, and staff to ensure exceptional customer service; and follow all federal, state, and local laws and Company policies. Criminal history may have a direct, adverse, and negative relationship with some of the material job duties of this position. These include the duties and responsibilities listed above, as well as the abilities to adhere to company policies, exercise sound judgment, effectively manage stress and work safely and respectfully with others, exhibit trustworthiness and professionalism, and safeguard business operations and the Company’s reputation. Pursuant to the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

This role requires scoping and creating project plans, planning and managing on\-the\-job learning programs including content design, development and measurement across multiple modalities and formats. The ideal candidate will have proven instructional design experience, strong communication skills and the ability to build strong relationships and influence key stakeholders. You will have a proven track record in taking on end\-to\-end ownership and successfully delivering results in a fast\-paced and dynamic business environment. This is an individual contributor role where cross\-functional partnerships will be key to success.  

  

Candidates should be prepared to share a portfolio during the interview process.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Manage projects amidst ambiguity, competing priorities, and challenging deadlines, adapting quickly to changing circumstances, processes and priorities
* Maintain a deep understanding of program and learner roles to ensure that learning solutions align with audience training needs
* Partner with subject matter experts and program stakeholders to identify learning goals and outcomes

Your day will be a dynamic blend of creative design, strategic planning, and collaborative problem\-solving. You'll dive into designing comprehensive learning programs, translating complex operational knowledge into clear, accessible content across multiple formats and platforms

Our Last Mile Training team is dedicated to developing world\-class learning experiences that support the growth and success of our frontline leaders in NA Last Mile Operations. title: Sr. EHS Program Manager, Safety Learning and Development
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


Amazon is looking for an enthusiastic Sr. Program Manager to join the Workplace Health and Safety (WHS), Safety Learning and Development (SLeD) team. We are not an average company, and this is certainly not your average position. This role offers you the opportunity to revolutionize safety training delivery worldwide. SLeD seeks an experienced, self\-motivated Program Manager who excels in a fast\-paced environment and has a solid background in program management. This role will support the deployment and management of safety training programs for operations associates and leadership globally.
  

  

As a Sr. Program Manager, you will work across teams to collaborate, generate buy\-in, develop and execute to a plan. You will drive the expansion and growth of our global safety training programs, scaling globally. You will play a crucial role in devising strategies, fostering partnerships, and executing initiatives to enhance the program's reach and impact. This entails serving as the main point of contact for stakeholders in coordinating and managing strategic, cross\-functional, and business\-critical programs and projects. The ideal candidate excels in crafting large\-scale global training program strategies, encompassing training design, roadmaps, gap analyses, financial planning, communication, and launch plans in a dynamic and diverse business environment. Effective communication and program management skills are essential, enabling you to manage multiple competing priorities and coordinate work across teams. The successful candidate will be proficient at working with data to identify trends, identify improvement opportunities, and proactively define plans to realize these opportunities.
  

To be successful in this role, the candidate will effectively multitask and prioritize, utilize data to encourage decisions, and maintain a high level of independence. They will be comfortable working in a growing and ambiguous work environment as we constantly improve and innovate. This leader must have solid written and verbal communications skills, the ability to influence stakeholders, and willingness to dive deep into details.  

  

  

  

Key job responsibilities  

Key job responsibilities  

* Accountable for the overall program strategy and planning for their regions/business units, communicate program requirements, negotiate priorities, and define success metrics
* Apply project management best practices to drive plan execution, mitigate risks, and improve effectiveness to deliver results in a timely manner
* Continuously improve program components and mechanisms to drive results and provide program status communication, deliver clear and concise summaries to leadership teams and effectively answer questions
* Serve as the primary point of contact for coordinating and managing strategic, cross\-functional, and business\-critical training programs and projects
* Build partnerships with key stakeholders and organizations to promote training program adoption.
* Effectively communicate and manage multiple, competing priorities while coordinating work across teams
* Define, track, and monitor progress against success metrics and project plans. Analyze and report on overall project health using key performance indicators and success metrics, and accurately and clearly report on status against program objectives

  

A day in the life  

Amazon offers a full range of benefits that support you and eligible family members, including domestic partners and their children. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment. If you are not sure that every qualification on the list above describes you exactly, we'd still love to hear from you! At Amazon, we value people with unique backgrounds, experiences, and skillsets. If you’re passionate about this role and want to make an impact on a global scale, please apply!

New Horizons In\-Home Care
  
Ready to build something meaningful?
  
At New Horizons, we support the caregivers who support our clients. Our People \& Culture team is growing, and we’re looking for a People \& Culture Generalist who wants more than just “processing HR” — someone who wants to help shape culture, strengthen leaders, and build better systems.

title: Human Resources Coordinator
description:
**Job Summary**  
The HR Coordinator supports day\-to\-day HR operations, ensuring a smooth employee experience throughout the employee’s lifecycle from recruiting and onboarding through data maintenance, compliance, and employee support. This role manages employee engagement, training, and benefits coordination.

**Job Responsibilities**

* Recruiting Support: Post jobs, schedule interviews, coordinate candidate communications, manage background checks, and reference checks.
* Onboarding \& Offboarding: Prepare offer letters, collect documentation, run new\-hire orientations, create employee files, coordinate equipment and systems access, process exits and recover assets.
* Education \& Training: Plan and execute the training requirements for all our staff in accordance with state, local and vendor standards.
* Talent \& Performance: Coordinate performance evaluation cycles. This includes 30, 60, 90\-day reviews, and annual reviews.
* HRIS \& Records: Maintain accurate employee records (personal data, status changes, compliance documents); assist with HRIS data entry, audits, and reporting.
* Benefits Administration: Support enrollments in medical, dental, 401k etc., life event changes, eligibility tracking; help answer employee questions; liaise with brokers/carriers as directed.
* Compliance: Assist with EEO, I‑9, E\-Verify, FMLA/leave tracking, required postings, training records, and policy administration.
* Employee Support: Respond to all HR inquiries; escalate complex issues to the HR Manager.
* Protected Leaves: Educate employees and process federal, WA \& CA protected leaves.
* Engagement \& Events: Help plan HR programs (recognition, engagement surveys, wellness, HR communications).
* Payroll Support: Submit changes (new hires, terminations, pay changes, deductions) to payroll before deadlines; validate reports.
* Reporting: Prepare routine HR metrics (headcount, turnover, time\-to\-fill, etc.).
* This job description includes the main duties and responsibilities of the job, but job duties are not limited to it, may perform other duties as assigned.

**Job Qualifications**

* 1\-2 years of HR support, recruiting coordination, or administrative experience.
* Knowledge of WA \& CA federal employment laws (WLAD, ADA, PFML, Paid Sick Leave, ACA).
* Experience with HRIS/ATS (e.g., Workday, UKG, WorkEasy, Paylocity) preferred.
* Preferred bilingual (English/Spanish)
* Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and discretion with confidential data.
* Excellent written and verbal communication; customer\-service mindset.
* Proficient in Microsoft 365\.

**Environment:**

* Extensive amounts to of time sitting at workstation
* Moderate amount of walking on concrete floors
* Must be able to lift up to 10 pounds
* Occasional work in extreme cold temperatures

Pay: From $21\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Vision insurance

Education:

* High school or equivalent (Required)

Language:

* Spanish (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

title: Senior Total Rewards Manager
description:
**About Helion**


We are a fusion power company based in Everett, WA, with the mission to build the world's first fusion power plant, enabling a future with unlimited clean electricity. Our vision is a world with clean, reliable, and affordable energy for everyone.


Since Helion's founding in 2013, we have raised over $1 billion from long\-time investors such as Sam Altman, Mithril, and Capricorn Investment Group as well as new investors SoftBank and Lightspeed to propel us forward. Our last prototype, Trenta, completed 10,000 high\-power pulses and reached plasma temperatures of 100 million degrees Celsius (9 keV). We are now operating Polaris, our next prototype on the path to the world's first fusion power plant.


This is a pivotal time to join Helion. You will tackle real\-world challenges with a team that prizes urgency, rigor, ownership, and a commitment to delivering hard truths – values essential to achieving what no one has before. Together, we will change the future of energy, because the world can't wait.

**What You Will Be Doing:**


You will own and evolve Helion’s total rewards function, building scalable, competitive cash, equity, and benefits programs that support a rapidly growing, technically complex organization. This role leads both strategy and execution: overseeing day\-to\-day total rewards operations while shaping long\-term compensation philosophy and design. You will drive compensation and equity planning cycles, refine our equity strategy, and strengthen employee understanding of our total rewards approach through clear education and communication. This is an onsite role reporting to the Director of People and Talent Operations in Everett, WA.


**You Will:**

* Own Helion’s total rewards strategy, including compensation, equity programs, and benefits, while partnering closely with HR, Finance, and Legal teams
* Lead and manage company\-wide compensation programs, including annual compensation cycles, promotions, performance\-based changes, and offer audits
* Own and evolve Helion’s equity compensation strategy, including equity plan design, burn\-rate management, grant guidelines, and administration
* Ensure compliance with applicable employment, pay equity, and benefit regulations across exempt and non\-exempt populations
* Develop and deliver ongoing compensation and equity education for employees, including training on performance, promotions, and equity value

**Required Skills:**

* 10\+ years of experience in compensation or total rewards, with demonstrated experience creating and executing company\-wide programs
* Proven ability of managing complex equity compensation programs within a Pre\-IPO, high\-growth startup, or technology environment
* Experience partnering with Talent and People Business Partners to create competitive compensation strategies that attract top talent while maintaining internal equity and fairness
* Hands\-on experience partnering with Finance teams to manage compensation planning, equity budgeting, forecasting, and internal equity analyses
* Demonstrated experience using data to inform processes and drive the adoption of total rewards programs

**Benefits**


Our total compensation package includes benefits, including but not limited to:

* Medical, Dental, and Vision plans for employees and their families
* 31 Days of PTO (21 vacation days and 10 sick days)
* 10 Paid holidays, plus company\-wide winter break
* Up to 5% employer 401(k) match
* Short term disability, long term disability, and life insurance
* Paid parental leave and support (up to 16 weeks)
* Annual wellness stipend

*Helion is an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. If you need assistance or an accommodation during the interview process, please let us know.*


Compensation Range: $200K \- $245K

description:
**Job Title: Director of Human Resources**  

**Reports to: CEO/President**

**Location: Seattle, WA (On\-site)**

**Pay range: $170,000\-$185,000/year (Salary, Exempt)**

**About Cochran**


Light up your career path and join Cochran, a trailblazer in the Pacific Northwest's electrical and technology construction scene. We are at the forefront of fostering innovation in the Pacific Northwest and beyond. As one of the largest woman\-owned contractors in the PNW,we take pride in **powering innovation, human well\-being, and community**. Our success is driven by the **extraordinary talent of our team members**, and we are committed to fostering a workplace where employees thrive.


Since 1954, Cochran has played a major role in designing and installing electrical and technological infrastructure throughout the Pacific Northwest and across the country. With expertise in electrical, security systems, audio/visual, communications, service, and maintenance, our dedication to fresh thinking and proven execution is full\-service.


Our values are proven, not explained:

* Be Transparent.
* Be Kind.
* Be Accountable.
* Be Respectful.
* Be Proactive.
* Be Inspired.
* Be Brilliant.

  

We've brought skylines, arenas, factories, even robots to life. While our technology evolves, our commitment to excellence never wavers. We're ready to light the way, will you join us?

**About the Role**


As the Director of Human Resources, you will lead the development and execution of people strategies that create a thriving, inclusive workplace. This role oversees HR, Payroll, and Talent \& Culture operations; ensures compliance with employment laws across multiple states; and champions employee engagement while aligning all HR initiatives with Cochran's mission, values, and long\-term business objectives.

**Essential Duties \& Responsibilities**

* Strategic Leadership \& Culture Building
	+ Serves as a trusted advisor to leadership, balancing organizational needs with employee well\-being.
	+ Partners with the Senior Management Team on roadmap planning, company initiatives, and change management.
* Employee Relations \& Conflict Resolution
	+ Provides empathetic, strategic guidance to leaders and HR/Payroll teams in navigating complex personnel matters.
	+ Oversee investigations, conflict resolution, disciplinary actions, and terminations with integrity and care.
	+ Upholds integrity in all HR records, ensuring documentation is respectful, consistent, and aligned with company values.
	+ Oversee matters related to Union/Collective Bargaining Agreement.
	+ Occasional travel as needed to regional offices for support and oversight.
* Compliance \& Policy Development
	+ Ensures adherence to federal, multi\-state, and local employment and payroll laws and regulations.
	+ Develops and maintains HR policies and procedures that reflect best practices and legal standards.
	+ Stays informed on HR trends, regulatory changes, and industry benchmarks.
	+ Oversee completion and submittal of the annual EEO\-1 Component 1 report in compliance with federal equal employment regulations.
	+ Partners with legal counsel as needed.
* Compensation \& Benefits Administration
	+ Oversee compensation and benefits programs, including health, dental, disability, life insurance, and leave policies.
	+ Responsible for the administration of the company 401(k) plan.
	+ Reviews and updates pay scale bands to ensure equity, transparency, and market competitiveness.
	+ Develops and oversees the department budget.
* Team Leadership \& Development
	+ Provides leadership and mentorship to the HR, Payroll, and Talent \& Culture teams, fostering a culture of collaboration, accountability, and continuous professional development.
	+ Oversees recruitment, engagement, and employee development efforts to ensure an inclusive, values\-aligned workplace experience.
	+ Supports internal training and development initiatives to build organizational capability.
	+ Models company's core values in daily interactions and decision\-making.
* Communication \& Advocacy
	+ Builds trust through clear, compassionate communication across all levels of the organization.
	+ Maintains a high degree of confidentiality and professionalism in all HR matters.
	+ Advocates for employees and leadership, ensuring fair representation and thoughtful resolution.

 **In this role, you will have the opportunity to:**

* Apply practical problem\-solving by identifying issues quickly and implementing effective solutions with confidence
* Deliver responsive and supportive service by addressing team estimating needs and pricing requests with reliability and follow\-through
* Strengthen technical expertise through continuous learning and proactive skill development
* Communicate clearly and persuasively, both in writing and speaking, while adapting messaging for different situations and audiences
* Foster a collaborative work environment by contributing to a positive, team\-first culture
* Ensure accuracy and drive continuous improvement by maintaining high quality standards and seeking opportunities to enhance processes
* Demonstrate strong leadership skills by providing clear directions, expectation setting, and consistently encouraging participation of team members

**We are looking for someone with the following competencies:**

* 7\+ years of progressive HR leadership experience, with a track record in strategic advising and culture building.
* SHRM\-SCP or SPHR preferred.
* Extensive knowledge of employment law, compliance, and policy development.
* Experience with Union/Collective Bargaining Agreements preferred. Experience within the electrical construction industry is a plus.
* Experience with Microsoft Office Suite; HRIS, ATS, Payroll software experience preferred.
* Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration or equivalent combination of education and experience.

**Why Cochran?**

***Company Benefits***


PTO \| 401k \| Medical, Dental \& Vision \| Life Insurance \| Seven Paid Holidays \| Voluntary Benefits \| EAP


Other compensation bonuses are eligible.

***DEIB Statement***


Cochran, Inc. provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

***Accessibility Statement***


This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training. Cochran, Inc. is committed to providing access, equal opportunity, and reasonable accommodation for individuals with disabilities in all aspects of employment, including the application process. To request a reasonable accommodation, **contact:** hr@cochraninc.com.


Compensation (Pay Range): **$170,000\-$185,000/year (Salary, Exempt)**

title: Senior HR Manager
description:
Join Grange Insurance Association, a well\-established leader in the insurance sector for over 130 years! Headquartered in the city of Seattle, with a footprint stretching across six Western states, we've been protecting families, farms, and businesses with unwavering commitment. As you embark on a fulfilling career with Grange, discover why our average employee tenure surpasses a decade. Make your mark with an organization that values quality, service, and the lasting impact you can bring!


We are looking for a dynamic leader to build and scale our **People function**. Reporting directly to the **Chief People and Strategy Officer**, you will be a architect of our human resources infrastructure and the go\-to expert for HR guidance across the organization.


This is a high\-impact role where you will balance high\-level strategic consulting with hands\-on operational support. You will serve as a trusted advisor to both staff and management, ensuring our culture and talent strategies align with our long\-term vision.


This position is located in Seattle and **requires a minimum of 3 days in office work.**


Here's a snapshot of what you'll be responsible for (Essential Functions):

### **Principal Duties \& Responsibilities**

* **Strategic Vision \& Execution:** Serve as an integral partner in defining our long\-term HR strategy. You will align people initiatives with broader business objectives to drive sustainable organizational growth.
* **Architect of Talent:** Design creative talent acquisition strategies that position us as an employer of choice. You will lead the reimagining of our onboardingprocess to ensure an engaging, impactful start for every new team member.
* **Performance Evolution:** Move us beyond "box\-ticking" by evolving the performance management cycle into a culture of continuous feedback, high\-stakes coaching, and meaningful career development.
* **Skills\-Based Inventory System:** Lead the design and deployment of a dynamic organizational skill and competency inventory to bridge the gap between our current capabilities and the skills required for the future.
* **Data\-Driven Workforce Planning:** Leverage turnover trends and talent metrics to proactively manage "mature workforce" transitions, ensuring the seamless transfer and preservation of institutional knowledge.
* **Total Rewards Innovation:** Support the evolution of our compensation and benefits philosophy, ensuring we remain highly competitive and attractive in the market.
* **Pioneer New Workflows:** Evaluate every aspect of the HR lifecycle—from payroll to compliance—implementing modern, tech\-forward improvements to increase efficiency and employee satisfaction.
* **Leadership Coaching:** Serve as a high\-level consultant to management, providing counsel on organizational design, risk mitigation, and complex employment actions.
* **Team Empowerment:** Lead, mentor, and elevate the HR department staff, fostering a culture of high performance and creative problem\-solving.
* **Risk \& Compliance Mastery:** Ensure the organization remains beyond reproach regarding Federal and State regulations, treating compliance as a vital foundation for ethical excellence.


Here is the experience we are looking for:

* We are looking for a "**builder**" who has a history of designing and launching original systems, resources, and frameworks from the ground up.
* A minimum of 8\+ years of progressive HR leadership experience (or equivalent expert\-level tenure). You have a proven record of moving beyond administration to organizational influence.
* Minimum of 3 years of Talent Acquisition experience
* Minimum of 3 years’ experience specifically focused on competency architecture.
* Minimum of 5 years of advanced talent and performance management experience.
* Minimum of 3 years’ experience in benefits administration and compliance, ERISA, and HIPAA. You possess a mastery of Federal and State labor laws, including specific expertise in Washington state regulations (FLSA, WA PFML, Equal Pay, ADA, and Opportunities Act).
* Advanced experience in leading learning and development strategies and programs
* Expert experience leading Payroll inclusive of using systems such as ADP and Workday.
* Advanced experience with MS applications, including confidence in consolidating data, V\-lookups, formula creation, and pivot tables.
* Experience with strategic workforce plannings.
* Experience in developing short and long\-term talent and business strategies.
* Must be self\-managed, able to work independently in an agile environment and well within a team to meet established deadlines.
* Advanced capabilities to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* High attention to detail and accuracy.
* Deals with confidential information and/or issues using discretion and judgment.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
* Ability to deal with ambiguity and demonstrate good judgment in resolving issues.


**Comprehensive Benefits:**

* Pay Range: $98,288 \- $190,795
* Flexible hybrid work schedule (3 days per week in Seattle or Spokane office required)
* Medical, Dental, and Vision plans
* 401(k) plan with up to 5% match
* Employer sponsored LTD, life insurance, and AD\&D
* Discretionary profit sharing and bonuses
* Fully subsidized ORCA card and/or free parking
* Education Reimbursement
* On\-Site Fitness Center
* Opportunities for career growth and advancement within the organization.
* A supportive and collaborative work environment.
* Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

  

If you are passionate about resources we provide to people to be their best and are ready to build, and are ready to join a dynamic team, we encourage you to apply.

 *Grange Insurance Association is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes all qualified candidates to apply.*

 *Grange Insurance Association is committed to ensuring a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees are treated with respect and dignity. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.*

title: HR Business Analyst, Customer Support and Operations
description:
**Job Description**

The UW Medicine Human Resources Workforce Management Systems (WMS) department is a shared services organization that supports all of UW Medicine Health System, including Harborview Medical Center (HMC), UW Medical Center\-Montlake Campus (UWMC\-Montlake), UW Medical Center\-Northwest Campus (UWMC\-NW), UW Neighborhood Clinics (UWNC), Faculty Practice Plan Svcs (FPPS), Airlift Northwest (ALNW), and UW Medicine Shared Services. WMS is responsible for the ongoing support and maintenance of HR infrastructure and applications which support all of these institutions, along with the implementation of new HR services and processes that are used to support and further the UW Medicine mission.


The HR Business Analyst for Customer Support and Operations (“Analyst”) position will report to the Director for UW Medicine HR (UWMHR) and operate as a member of the Workforce Management Systems Team that is dedicated to supporting UW Medicine HR projects, processes, workforce data management and reporting.


The Analyst will serve as the primary customer support coordinator across the UW Medicine HR intake points (e.g. hrwms inbox, UW Connect), monitoring and facilitating the management of complex, cross\-functional inquiries/issues in accordance with established protocols as well as ensuring assigned emails receive prompt and complete replies. The Analyst will work with leaders from across the Health System, HR staff and customers to ensure that the WMS team consistently meet the needs of our customers.


Additionally, the Analyst will support our internal HR team and other internal business partners with on\-going maintenance, operational and technical support of all aspects of the Workday HCM and Workday Recruiting system, including but not limited to pay period end auditing and issue resolution and testing support for regular and ad hoc Workday updates.


**Position Responsibilities**

HR Analyst \- Customer Service Coordinator and Operations Support:


* Develop extensive insight across UW Medicine HR processes (both within and external to Workday HCM, including Workday Recruiting) and consistently apply this end\-to\-end perspective in conjunction with meeting the service delivery objectives of the WMS team.
* Serve as primary resource for monitoring inquiries coming in to and assigned to the WMS team (via UW Connect, hrwms inbox), facilitating management of inquiries/issues in accordance with established protocols.
* Serve as an escalated point of contact for Workday HCM (including Workday Recruiting) administrative issues and inquiries, provide advanced consultative expertise to support the WMS team by resolving complex HR and Payroll inquiries.
* Serve as primary resource for a defined scope of centralized Human Capital Management (HCM) system transactions, including Workday Recruiting.
* Coordinate with UW HR HCM Operations staff and with external Central Business Unit experts to address escalated inquiries
* Execute critical pay period end audits via established Workday reports; complete initial review, follow\-up with managers/recruiters/WMS team to determine next steps, and track through to resolution prior to payroll cut\-off.
* Have proficient knowledge of policy, procedure, and Workday fundamentals to complete routine audits of the system.
* Serve as back\-up support for Workday HR Analysts, as needed, for complex HCM administrative issues and inquiries, providing advanced consultative expertise to support UW Medicine Health System staff and working students.
* Provide support in managing duplicate candidate records in Workday Recruiting, including conducting research and coordinating follow\-up with the Recruitment team as needed. Develop Duplicate Management Framework subject matter expertise, becoming primary expert for WMS Team.
* Effectively escalate issues to supervisor when normal business practices are not successful in resolving.
* Identify and execute process improvement opportunities to deliver enhanced customer services to the organization.
* Develop and maintain tools, templates, desk procedure documentation, standards and reports to support HR Analyst functions.
* Identify and develop relevant content for organization\-specific training and development that may be added

to the working repository of knowledge content. Assist in training any new HR Analysts that join the WMS Team.
  

* Apply HCM subject matter expertise to effectively execute and support Workday HCM testing activities, including test planning and documentation.
* Contribute to an outstanding customer\-service environment, including strong collaboration with key stakeholders.
* Apply tact and diplomacy when dealing with customers, establishing credibility and building trusting relationships with business owners, customers, and leadership.
* Develop constructive alignments with designated areas of the organization in the interest of delivering high quality services.
Other Duties as Assigned:  



  

* Support periodic Workday update processes, including HCM testing activities (planning and documentation).
* Support WMS and Workday HCM knowledge management processes, including identification and verification of additions/changes to the working repository of knowledge content.
* Support WMS training activities, including development and verification of relevant content.
* Attend team and departmental meetings as appropriate.
**Minimum Qualifications**

  

Bachelor's Degree in a technology, business, or human resources related field.


A minimum of three years of relevant work experience to include:


* Experience working in the HR, Benefits and/or Payroll domains
* Working experience processing and troubleshooting transactions in HCM systems
* Experience drafting and maintaining process and procedure documentation
* Experience collaborating with department transactional initiators and understanding the political landscape amongst distributed organizations
* Extensive experience interacting with customers, including resolution of complex inquiries and efficient performance during high\-volume time\-frames
* Experience supporting system testing and/or data validation activities
* Ability to learn and apply basic Workday Reporting skills

*Equivalent education and/or experience may substitute for minimum qualifications except when there are legal requirements, such as a license, certification, and/or registration.*

**Desired**

* 2\+ years hands\-on Workday functional experience in HCM
* 1\+ experience with Workday reporting
* Project Management \& Organization (Small to Medium sized efforts)

**Working Environmental Conditions**

* The work may be performed during the department’s core business hours; either by telecommuting or in an office environment, based on the team’s business requirements and manager approval. Typically hybrid telework arrangement (2 days a week in office/3 days remote).
* Some off\-hours and weekends may be needed to resolve problems and respond to emergencies.
* Is expected to meet in\-person as directed by manager.
* Because of the physically separated sites for UW Medicine, this position requires the ability to travel to alternative work locations, as needed.

**Compensation, Benefits and Position Details**

**Pay Range Minimum:**

$81,600\.00 annual
**Pay Range Maximum:**

$87,600\.00 annual
**Other Compensation:**

* 

**Benefits:**

For information about benefits for this position, visit https://www.washington.edu/jobs/benefits\-for\-uw\-staff/
**Shift:**

First Shift (United States of America)
**Temporary or Regular?**

This is a regular position
**FTE (Full\-Time Equivalent):**

100\.00%
**Union/Bargaining Unit:**

Not Applicable
**About the UW**

Working at the University of Washington provides a unique opportunity to change lives – on our campuses, in our state and around the world.


UW employees bring their boundless energy, creative problem\-solving skills and dedication to building stronger minds and a healthier world. In return, they enjoy outstanding benefits, opportunities for professional growth and the chance to work in an environment known for its diversity, intellectual excitement, artistic pursuits and natural beauty.


**Our Commitment**

The University of Washington is committed to fostering an inclusive, respectful and welcoming community for all. As an equal opportunity employer, the University considers applicants for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, national origin, citizenship, sex, pregnancy, age, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, disability, or veteran status consistent with UW Executive Order No. 81 .


To request disability accommodation in the application process, contact the Disability Services Office at 206\-543\-6450 or dso@uw.edu .


Applicants considered for this position will be required to disclose if they are the subject of any substantiated findings or current investigations related to sexual misconduct at their current employment and past employment. Disclosure is required under Washington state law .

title: Accounting and HR Assistant
description:
**Benefits:**
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Free food \& snacks
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance
**About Us**  

At Cassiel, Inc. dba Visiting Angels, we believe the best teams are built on genuine people, meaningful work, and plenty of shared laughter along the way. Our culture balances professionalism with personality—we work hard, we care deeply about our clients and coworkers, and we don’t take ourselves too seriously. You’ll find an office where dedication and humor live side by side, and where being part of the team means bringing both your skills and your personality to the table.  

Our mission says it best: *“We provide a profoundly remarkable experience.”* That’s not just for our clients—it’s for our employees, too.  

The Role  

We’re looking for an **Accounting \& HR Assistant** to support both accounting and human resources functions across multiple business locations. In this role, you’ll be the behind\-the\-scenes hero—keeping payroll accurate, billing smooth, benefits organized, and processes humming.  

This is the perfect opportunity for someone who loves variety, thrives on details, and enjoys being part of a team that blends excellence with humor.  

**Accounting Functions**
* Process client activations/deactivations in QuickBooks, WellSky, and billing spreadsheets.
* Ensure billing accuracy, resolve issues, and process invoices for clients, LTC, and Veterans Affairs.
* Process and reconcile payroll through APS (eSELFSERVE).
* Review accounts receivable, contact clients on past due balances, and process payments.

**HR Functions**
* Administer employee benefit plans, including enrollments, ACA reporting, and terminations.
* Manage the APS benefits console.
* Maintain and update HR forms (digital/fillable versions).
* Support HR programs and assist with process improvements.

**Team \& Leadership**
* Serve as backup for payroll and A/R processing when needed.
* Collaborate with colleagues and leadership to promote organizational effectiveness.
* Foster a culture of care, excellence, and teamwork.

What We’re Looking For
* Some experience in accounting and HR (QuickBooks, APS, WellSky \= big plus).
* Strong computer and organizational skills.
* Excellent communication (written and spoken).
* High ethical standards and ability to maintain confidentiality.
* Ability to juggle priorities with accuracy and grace.
* A collaborative spirit—and an appreciation for humor along the way.

Why You’ll Love Working Here
* A supportive, fun\-loving team that feels like family.
* Leadership that values your input and growth.
* Work that matters—supporting caregivers and clients in meaningful ways.
* A culture that mixes dedication with plenty of laughter.
* Competitive pay, benefits, and opportunities to grow.

**Ready to Apply?**  

If you’re looking for more than *just a job*—if you want a workplace where your skills are valued and your personality is welcomed—apply today. Bring your talent, your drive, and maybe even your sense of humor. We can’t wait to meet you!

title: Regional Director of Human Resources
description:
**Regional Human Resources Director**
-------------------------------------

**Hybrid Role (Remote / In\-Person)**  

**Required Travel:** Oregon \& Southern Washington

### **Position Overview**


As the **Regional Human Resources Director**, you will play a pivotal role in shaping and executing the HR strategy for our long\-term care facilities across the region. This **hybrid position** combines remote work with in\-person facility support and **regular travel throughout Oregon \& Southern Washington.**


You will oversee HR operations, ensure compliance with employment laws and regulatory requirements, and foster a positive, engaged culture that supports and values our healthcare professionals.

### **Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field (HR certification a plus)
* Minimum **5\+ years of HR experience** in a long\-term care or healthcare setting
* Proven leadership experience in multi\-site HR operations
* Strong knowledge of HR principles, employment laws, and regulatory compliance
* Excellent leadership, strategic thinking, and interpersonal skills
* Ability to travel regularly within Oregon and Southern Washington.

### **Major Duties \& Responsibilities**

**Strategic HR Leadership**

* Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with organizational goals
* Partner with executive leadership on workforce planning, talent acquisition, and retention initiatives

**Employee Relations**

* Provide guidance and support to on\-site HR teams regarding employee relations matters
* Ensure consistent application of company policies and procedures across all facilities

**Talent Management**

* Oversee recruitment and onboarding for key leadership and hard\-to\-fill roles
* Implement development programs to support employee growth and career progression

**Compliance Management**

* Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws

**Performance Management**

* Lead performance management processes, including goal setting and evaluations
* Partner with leadership to address performance issues and corrective action plans

**Employee Engagement**

* Develop and implement initiatives to enhance engagement, retention, and satisfaction
* Serve as a trusted liaison between employees and management

**Collaboration \& Communication**

* Build strong partnerships with facility administrators, managers, and corporate HR
* Communicate HR policies, programs, and initiatives clearly across all levels

### **Compensation \& Benefits**


Compensation is based on qualifications, skills, and experience and may vary within the stated range.

**Exciting Benefits Include:**

* Competitive salary based on experience
* Generous Paid Time Off
* 401(k) retirement plan
* Flexible Daily Pay options
* Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance
* Wellness program access
* Inclusive and collaborative workplace culture
